--- Thread (50 messages, 2018-04-10) ---
[2018-04-10 14:27] Deleted User (Deleted User): bkQId-UlNwB What did i do wrong this game? Did i just analyze the set incorrectly or should i have gone with one or two more venges?
[2018-04-10 14:27] p0lari (Polari): that doesn't look like a set where you want to venges
[2018-04-10 14:28] Deleted User (Deleted User): I went for venges with one infusion grid
[2018-04-10 14:28] Deleted User (Deleted User): I ended up getting beat by Urban Sentry, Gauss Cannon and Wall u_u
[2018-04-10 14:29] p0lari (Polari): yeah that's how you lose when you go too aggro
[2018-04-10 14:30] Deleted User (Deleted User): I'm just playing horribly today
[2018-04-10 14:30] p0lari (Polari): uh I'm not great at venges either so I'm not super confident in laying out rules, but like
[2018-04-10 14:31] p0lari (Polari): with venge you get a large spike of damage at the point when you cash in your drones, and you're breachproof afterwards
[2018-04-10 14:31] p0lari (Polari): those are its strengths
[2018-04-10 14:32] p0lari (Polari): if the opponent can defend, it's just going to get worse for you as you no longer have econ and will just slowly whittle down
[2018-04-10 14:32] Deleted User (Deleted User): Yep thats exactly what happened
[2018-04-10 14:32] darkeisbein: looks like a sentry > deemer > plasma set to me
[2018-04-10 14:32] darkeisbein: seems like such a nice natural transition in that tech
[2018-04-10 14:32] Deleted User (Deleted User): I kept attacking for 11 against his walls and sentries, felt bad
[2018-04-10 14:32] p0lari (Polari): in this pool the opponent is going to go gb with infusion grid for defense
[2018-04-10 14:33] p0lari (Polari): on top of base set stuff
[2018-04-10 14:33] darkeisbein: oh right infusion is in there aswell
[2018-04-10 14:33] darkeisbein: yeah no way it's venge
[2018-04-10 14:33] darkeisbein: for anything other then a lategame greensink
[2018-04-10 14:33] darkeisbein: and then click it to sink green
[2018-04-10 14:33] Deleted User (Deleted User): I just need to play more to learn what beats what i guess
[2018-04-10 14:33] p0lari (Polari): all-in with only four venges and nothing else is questionable to begin with
[2018-04-10 14:34] darkeisbein: venge converts 3 gold production to 2 attack production which isn't all that great if you can use that gold to buy efficient defense
[2018-04-10 14:34] darkeisbein: because that means the value of gold is actually pretty good
[2018-04-10 14:34] p0lari (Polari): it's just not enough damage to make the opponent sweat unless they went low econ red
[2018-04-10 14:34] darkeisbein: and with infusion grid 3 gold is worth a lot
[2018-04-10 14:34] awaclus: >venge converts 3 gold production to 2 attack production which isn't all that great
well, it would be pretty great if it was just that, but it also costs resources
[2018-04-10 14:35] p0lari (Polari): though in this case if you did go for a later timing, the opponent could have got redeemer or plasma for attack and bought infu grids more easily
[2018-04-10 14:35] p0lari (Polari): also your blastforge was really bad
[2018-04-10 14:35] Deleted User (Deleted User): I should have either built it earlier or not at all
[2018-04-10 14:35] darkeisbein: I mean infusion grid cost ~ 6 gold so 3 gold is worth ~ 2 defense
[2018-04-10 14:36] darkeisbein: and yeah that math is kinda wonky 😛
[2018-04-10 14:36] p0lari (Polari): you should not have got it earlier, it would have done nothing
[2018-04-10 14:36] darkeisbein: basically you only venge rush if there are no good defensive tools in the set
[2018-04-10 14:36] p0lari (Polari): or hmm maybe sentries would have been decent
[2018-04-10 14:37] darkeisbein: or if you can go bp venges with something like trinity drone
[2018-04-10 14:37] p0lari (Polari): in general one of the harder questions in a venge game is do you build early attack and do you get forge
[2018-04-10 14:37] darkeisbein: or ebb turbine + synth I guess?
[2018-04-10 14:38] p0lari (Polari): like if you get a goose cannon early it's annoying for someone planning to go straight for a big venge all-in, picking at your engineers before you're ready
[2018-04-10 14:38] Deleted User (Deleted User): It seems to be at least a little bp
[2018-04-10 14:38] p0lari (Polari): but on the other hand it slows your own buildup a lot too so maybe the all-in player wins anyway
[2018-04-10 14:38] p0lari (Polari): if both are taking it slow then forge and a wall for some early absorb pretty quickly pays back for itself
[2018-04-10 14:38] darkeisbein: I mean it is somewhat bp yeah
[2018-04-10 14:39] darkeisbein: but it's less bp then it seems
[2018-04-10 14:39] p0lari (Polari): but on the other hand it slows you down even further so if you got it against one goose and the opponent then continues to build up drones and conduits without you stopping them, you're screwed again
[2018-04-10 14:39] darkeisbein: like you can become bp vs. small breaches by dynamoing your whole drone line
[2018-04-10 14:39] p0lari (Polari): but anyway in this game you got forge right as you transitioned to venges anyway
[2018-04-10 14:40] p0lari (Polari): so it didn't really do anything as you weren't all that vulnerable to begin with
[2018-04-10 14:40] p0lari (Polari): you would have been better off spending that gold on gooses instead
[2018-04-10 14:41] Deleted User (Deleted User): That makes a lot of sense

[2018-04-11 17:05] darkeisbein: Can someone explain to me how to play with auric impulse?
[2018-04-11 17:05] darkeisbein: currently I'm mostly in never buy auric mode because it confuses me to no end
[2018-04-11 17:06] awaclus: it's a drone that you're forced to resell into the supply as soon as you run out of engineers or auri supply
[2018-04-11 17:07] darkeisbein: that seems worse then a regular drone ... like I get that it can kinda extend your drone supply
[2018-04-11 17:07] darkeisbein: but that is only relvevant in games where you use up all drone supply
[2018-04-11 17:07] darkeisbein: which is probably not the only usecase for auric
[2018-04-11 17:09] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): It's good for winding up to big tech purchases, in cases where you don't want drones.  For things like a faster Lucina etc.  It's like a colorless ebb click that you have to plan out ahead of time
[2018-04-11 17:13] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): The idea being that it would replace the last couple drones you buy: 14 drones vs 12 drones and buying 2 aurics per turn.  So you keep the option to sell those last 2 fake drones off for a burst of gold on a critical turn
[2018-04-11 17:14] .bky_1556 (bky): You also use Auric whenever you'd otherwise float 3 and have energy available.
[2018-04-11 17:15] .bky_1556 (bky): Particularly when it's sufficiently late in the game that you don't want to commit to +1 drone.
[2018-04-11 17:16] .bky_1556 (bky): Aurics are a notably strong set-up for an Antima Comet.
[2018-04-11 17:17] kristian1103 (Arkanishu): auric is good if you want the option to sell your drone instead of committing to buy one
[2018-04-11 17:19] .bky_1556 (bky): I guess my point is that you don't need to *chain* Aurics.

[2018-04-11 18:25] atma_w (Atma): guys, I have a question, is there a point of ever buying 12+ gold units? I never have enough gold to buy them after getting defence each turn.
[2018-04-11 18:32] awaclus: you just have to plan your build such that you get to the expensive unit relatively fast
[2018-04-11 18:32] awaclus: so that you can get it while you're still absorbing most/all of your opponent's damage
[2018-04-11 18:33] atma_w (Atma): so you only get 1 expensive unit ever?
[2018-04-11 18:33] awaclus: well, that depends on what it is
[2018-04-11 18:34] p0lari (Polari): if it's a 1-stock unit like many expensive ones are, and it's the only expensive thing in set, then yes :LongNoseLiar:
[2018-04-11 18:34] awaclus: if it's e.g. Omega Splitter, you can use your previous absorber as soak and absorb on Omega instead to let you get another one
[2018-04-11 18:36] awaclus: if it's Lucina, then it's probably the only expensive unit you're getting
[2018-04-11 18:37] awaclus: in general, big absorbers make it easier to go for expensive units
[2018-04-11 18:46] .bky_1556 (bky): For Tatsu, the opponent's goal is likely to prevent you from buying a second without losing the first.
